response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an experienced full-stack software engineer with a solid track record in web, mobile, and backend development, but his profile has virtually no overlap with the Principal Performance Architect role. The position requires deep expertise in SoC/IP modeling, hardware-software co-design, AI accelerator optimization, and GPU/SIMD architecture — none of which appear in his resume. His most hardware-adjacent experience is limited to robotic arms in Java/DLang and a bachelor's thesis on wave field synthesis with C++. While he has some AI agent infrastructure exposure at re:cinq and low-latency programming at Sociomantic, these do not translate to the hardware architecture and deep-learning workload optimization skills this role demands.
strengths: 14+ years of software engineering experience covering multiple layers of the stack, satisfying the BS+8yr education requirement
Low-latency systems work at Sociomantic using async I/O, fibers, and high-performance databases demonstrates performance-awareness
Exposure to AI agent infrastructure and MCP frameworks at re:cinq shows current engagement with AI tooling
concerns: Zero experience with SoC/IP modeling, hardware simulation, or chip architecture — the core technical requirement of the role
No background in GPU architecture, SIMD/MIMD design, or deep neural network training/inference optimization
Entire career is software-only (web, backend, mobile); no hardware-software co-design or AI accelerator work of any kind
